reviews.review_description:
When I started playing, I was frustrated because I hadn't quite mastered the rhythm. Then, after practicing, I found my weapons (Persephone and the Hounds) and was finally able to keep my score around x16. That's a truly huge achievement and I was really glad! By the way, the game has a rhythm mode, and I used it for some difficult challenges. Only the sword feels like a soft stick, while the other weapons feel heavy and dangerous.
Overall, the game has a unique style, to me, it reminds me of Darksiders. Dante's Circle of Hells looks beautiful even in the metaquest and every level is truly unique. There aren't a lot of enemy variety in the game, but there are enough throughout the game (around 10-12 hours with challenges).  The Red Judge boss fights is really good, and you need to use different cues in each fight.
And finally, the music – IT'S AMAZING. The ending track during the final boss fight (featuring Serj Tankian) is a masterpiece. I often listen to the soundtrack from this game on YouTube.
You have to buy this game)
I played the game less than an hour and had it for only 3 days and my refund was denied to this game.

Seriously, wtf am I supposed to do in this?? You got a crap tutorial that constantly pauses you as you're getting into it and throws more directions at you which confuses the hell out of me, freezing my freedom of movement, while trying to keep up with a beat as the headset is trying to process the lagging frame rates. I heard it was great on PC and consoles, I'll stick with that. No wonder it was on sale for $5, the PS2 looks better than this.

reviews.review_title: Good in Flat, bad VR port
reviews.review_description: Whilst Metal: Hellsinger is by no means a bad game the VR version seems very boring in comparison. The controls are very counterintuitive. The flick to reload mechanic is very badly implemented and stops the whole flow of this so called rhythm shooter. As a result none of the weapons are fun to use. The game is fun for about 15 minutes after which you realize how it's held back by it's clunky controls and badly implemented mechanics. Do yourself a favor and skip the VR version of this game.

This FPS is amazing on console and it's remarkably better in VR. I was hesitant because I didn't want this to sour how much I enjoy playing this on my PS5, but I'm so glad I picked it up. It takes some time to master, but it's somehow easier to maneuver in 3D. Absolutely no complaints. More, please!

Not for me. Nicely presented but felt a bit clunky to control and the enemies got very dull fast. Confusing game play, X button is an attack press and also pauses the game on a long press! That’s just insane design when the controller has a dedicated pause button. It probably gets better but i was so underwhelmed that I am refunding after about 15 minutes
Amazing game. highly recommend if you love Metal And The Doom Games. honestly we need more shooters that bring aspects of rhythm into them.

Very good, high-quality game. This is literally the best rhythm game for Quest and one of the best old-school shooters overall. If you love Doom, Quake, and adrenaline, you should definitely give it a try.

in common great game: music, metal, rhythmic, dynamic!!

but VR version (the only I have myself) has some annoying issues:
1) the Skull super-attack is activated out of rhythm
1.2) the skull target is not correct very much (and some others little bit)
2) super-kill acts and sounds out of rhythm
3) sword attack is bugy, freezy and partially out of rhythm
4) some count of mobs and/or fireballs create continuous micro-freezes
5) translation on FB creates record where  effects have huge delay. But in-game they look and sound in time

I want to love this game, the thought of playing one of my favorite rhythm action games on PS5 in VR is just fantastic, but I can't for the life of me get the calibration down correctly.

I find i tap on the music's beat, and the arrow isn't lined up with the beat sync marker, I tried calibrating three separate times, and nothing worked, which is making it hard to enjoy the game and its soundtrack.
You should definitely get this thing as one of the best games ever I’ve ever played on VR because it good
Flawless port and amazing game but more licenced music packs could make it better .

My favorite part of this game is the music, so my review has some bias. I love metal and have picked up all the metal dlcs. The music intensifies as you progress through the level, which fits the game so well. 

This is a rhythm based shooter game, you don't have to play to the rhythm but it feels more rewarding to see you kills unfold to the beat, plus you receive more points if you play to the rhythm.

The environment is beautiful, playing in various hellscapes filled with demons and monsters to shoot.

The downfall are the controls, it takes a little while to get used to and even when I revisit the game I feel like I need a refresher on the controls. Thankfully every other aspect of this game is so good, that I'll  struggle to give it anything less than 5 stars.

Controls are awful entirely unintuitive, it's a game where you need to keep moving and it does everything on the same control as the movement meaning you have to stop to reload and activate special attacks. The swap weapon button is the movement stick meaning if like me you grip the controller tight your gonna constantly be swapping when you don't want to, the other control jumps and dashes all movement things that should really be with the movement stick, desperately needs a way to reprogram controls. It's a shame because I loved the non vr game
GREAT SOUNDTRACK, GREAT STORY, GREAT GAMEPLAY, but one question, how do I access purgatory on vr?

Update: 

Months later and still no patch. It’s abandoned. But it’s so much fun I have to raise my score to five stars anyway. There are so few games that dare to have gameplay this intense and chaotic in VR. It really stands out as something very special.

Original Review:

Five stars for the art direction and intense combat, three stars for the technical implementation, four stars overall.

First off, ignore any reviews based on this game’s “future potential” because all signs indicate that this was a contract job and once the game was released the contract was complete and the code was abandoned. So what you get is likely all you’ll ever get.

Now that that’s out of the way, what’s here right now is a total blast to play if you can deal with some sloppy, unfinished elements.

There’s been a lot of criticism regarding the graphics. It looks like a Quest 2 game even if you’re running on a Quest 3. The visuals are greatly scaled down from the flat screen version and it uses ASW so it has the usual ASW visual artifacts.

If you’ve ever wanted to fight your way through a Hell populated with demons that look like Todd McFarlane figures come to life then hopefully you can look past the rendering technology to let the beautiful art direction shine through. As Quest games go, this is one of the better looking ones because of its skillful art direction.

I didn’t think I would enjoy the music since I’m more into nu metal bands than whatever this metal sub-genre is, but within the context of the game it’s great. The music is dynamic and as you level up during the battles vocals will kick in. Just like the art direction, the soundtrack is very well executed.

Unfortunately certain aspects of the game feel unfinished. The tutorials are sparse and sometimes confusing. The UI interactions in the hub world are super janky and frustrating. It’s difficult to get the selection buttons, (like the leaderboard display), to work when you click them. Picking up the books you use to change settings is also difficult, but on the plus side there are lots of settings you can tweak to customize the game once you do manage to grab a book.

The gameplay is a chaotic adrenaline rush and a hell of a lot of fun.  Leaping through the air, dashing, and doing old school platform game double jumps while spinning around and targeting enemies is a total blast! (I highly recommend playing while standing and turning with your feet if you have the space.)

However some people strongly dislike the controls. It feels like a flat to VR port which it is, but aside from the finishing attack triggered by the X button which always feels disjointed, I’ve gotten used to the controls.  You need to try to stay calm and focused and not overwork the thumb sticks since clicking them down activates the dash and weapon selection wheel.  Being calm and focused is generally better anyway when the combat gets intense.

I’ve played through several levels so far and the bits of story provided by introductory cut scenes and the narrator are entertaining. The bosses are challenging and feel like old school arcade boss battles. I haven’t played all the levels yet because I keep going back and replaying earlier levels.  Like any rhythm game the replayabilty is very high.

This is the kind of action I was hoping I would get from Hellsweeper and Dead Hook. Hellsweeper is an excellent game that has strong support from the developers, but its complex game mechanics have kept me from getting totally immersed. (I’m going to keep trying though.) Dead Hook’s environments never hooked me.

I waited for a sale and bought Metal Hellsinger during a 40% off promotion, but I would have been fine paying full price now that I’ve played it.

A few tips:

If you play with the built in speakers, skip the audio calibration.

Attack to the beat. For example if you swing the sword off beat it will do almost no damage.  This is a rhythm game first and foremost, but you can disable the need to attack on the beat in the settings and turn it into just a combat game, (although your scores won’t make it to the leaderboards).

And again, try to stay calm and focused no matter how hellish it gets down there. 😁

Usually I play this game on Series X but got it again during black friday sales. This game is like Doom, awesome music I wish Mick Gordon did some songs for this game.

Dev Feedback:  please add an option to increase smooth turning sensitivity, I feel it's so slow currently.

So I cannot keep a beat very well but this game gives you the option to make every shot seem like it's on beat and that's how I play . And I turn the HUD off so it's more immersive and then it's just a fast paced VR shooter almost like Doom 2016 or doom eternal. So if rhythm shooters aren't your thing you can still have a blast in this game .
If you play like this I recommend during the difficulty settings to the hardest possible that way it still challenging. If you are a doom addict like me I highly recommend getting this game .

There are too many VR games that do everything this game does, but better. The melee feels terrible, no weight, no intensity…did they even play Until You Fall!? Gun play is okay but did they even play Walking Dead?! Take notes from the games that did it right!!! There is an experience to be had in here somewhere but it needs a lot of polish and I haven’t seen any updates since release so I’m not getting my hopes up. 

Don’t give them your money and time unless they sink some more effort into this one. Now…back to Batman!

reviews.review_title: With tweaks, could be a benchmark for vr
reviews.review_description:
Like others say this game is so so good.  Music hits hard and really drives up the action and that action is top notch.  

Needs tweaks to the weapons - the boomerangs are worthless, and really only the two pistols, skull and regular shotgun allow you to build combos.  Not even the sword is usable for anything other than the finishing move.  They could honestly delete all other weapons and just build combos around the three basic guns. 

Lastly, more enemy variety is needed.  Personally I'd like if there were certain enemies with ballistic shields requiring you to do combo sequences with the sword like until you fall.  That would make this game a real goat.  

This is still a must play VR experience regardless of these small flaws.  One of the best VR games ever made imo in terms of taking VR mechanics and movement to the max.  If the devs keep releasing updates it should elevate to a permanent classic for VR.

What can I say about this game

Wow.. first...well done guys 

Graphically  amazingly smooth, walking and jumps and double jumps !!
Gameplay is amazing great tutorials ..music pumps,  you  feel the  guns  hit hard .wasn't hard to pick up and get those pints racking 
I'm in my 50s and this rocks !!!!

Tried first time today, disappointed. Not only are the control instructions displaying the wrong buttons to press, but the breaker is when going through the portal for tutorial, it goes no further as you cant select play. Wasted money. May edit once fixed.

reviews.review_title: Definitely what it claims to be
reviews.review_description: I've been off vr about since I bought the quest 3  cuz I really miss old PC VR but I keep trying and this is one of the first games in a long time that I think I'm going to spend a pretty good amount of time with. I like it enough that I'm going to buy the psvr2 version for the upgrade and graphics. I however did not like being unable to change my controls until the third mission the on-screen tutorials locked me out from being able to do that.

This plays just like the PlayStation version except it’s more immersive and so far no bugs or problems with gameplay, only thing that could be better is the lighting in some areas and maybe a graphics update for the quest 3 one day. But overall a very fun game!

reviews.review_title: A boring port of a flat screen game.
reviews.review_description:
To whoever is reading this, if you were a fan of the original Metal Hellsinger on PlayStation and Xbox and you want to play what is effectively just a port of the game in VR, give it a shot. Otherwise if you are looking for an actually immersive VR game, look elsewhere because this is definitely not it.

I really tried to enjoy this game, I really did. I’m not just making this review negative just to be like one of those guys who will give a 1 star and then make less than 5 words and not explain anything beyond said words. 

When I first started up the game, I was met with a tutorial and instructions that absolutely would not leave me alone. Things were not only poorly explained but I felt like I was reading a Harry Potter book just so I could get to playing the game. And when I DID get to playing the game, I THEN met the second horseman of the apocalypse… The physics.

The physics are just bad. I’m not even gonna sugar coat it. This is yet ANOTHER VR game where you can just lightly tap an enemy with your sword and they will be given massive damage. (For the developer’s sake I hope that Mr Vr Voice doesn’t make a video on this game, he will tear this game to shreds just like he did with Battle Talent.) 

The game also pads itself out to no end. Do you like doing some bare bones platforming in a level and then be stopped in front of a wall and be forced to destroy a hundred demons before proceeding in a level that honestly feels like the same as the rest of the game? Play Metal Hellsinger.

What did I like about the game? I like that you aren’t restricted to staying on the ground to fight the boss. I love that not only do you have a dash AND a jump button, but you can “soar” though the level… and my that I mean you are just galloping like a horse if you want to speed run the level… it’s repetitive just like the enemy rush. 

I think what I hate the most about the game is the fact that the gimmicks of the bosses are THAT pathetic. In level 3 the boss has a force field and is like “HAHAHA YOU WILL NEVER PENETRATE MY BARRIER!” Even though you can just walk underneath it and just shoot the boss anyway. It’s like the boss isn’t even trying to kill me. 

If you want to play an immersive VR game where you are in Hell fighting demons to the death repeatedly, just play HellSweeper VR. It’s everything that THIS game is but a million times better, minus having the rhythm aspect.
I took the day off from work to play this game and I love it! I'm a Metal head and love arcade style games so this is perfect!!

It’s like if pistol whip had free movement, the music was metal and the enemies were demons.

Just needs bhaptics support. It would work really well with this game

A bit of Doom's DNA, a dash of heavy metal, and some awesome voice acting with just the right amount of cheesiness. I tried the original for about an hour and thought, 'It's okay, but this would be insane in VR.' Then they released the VR demo, and it confirmed everything I was thinking. I remember the PCVR demo being fun, but the full standalone version is everything I wanted. With this game and Hellsweeper, I finally got my 'Doom-like rush' experiences in VR.

In my opinion, THIS is the way to play the game. The concept was made for VR.

reviews.review_title: Great, but  could use one more option.
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE:- After playing for a while now, I've found some big & small issues that bring my experience with the game down a notch.

1- though it may ONLY be for the side-missions (torments?), but I've noticed that even if I choose another song before entering (even ones I paid nearly $15 extra getting), I still get the exact same song mission. Addingva small layer of repetition to something that can end up being tedious already when it comes to the harder torments that I have to keep repeating due to failure. Which brings me to my next bigger issue.. 

2- There being no "Retry" option after mission failure. It ALWAYS takes you back to the hub with no option to do anything but wait for that to load. Select the Hell again. Enter the portal again. And wait for THAT to load just to try again... and god forbid you fail AGAIN...
But now for the BIGGEST and most inexcusable issue in a game that REQUIRES speed and rythm...

3- Switching weapons, outside of the instant swap between the very last weapon used, is extremely counter-intuitive to speed & tempo. There is hardly time to fight a menu you have to already hold a button down for on top of moving your hand & praying it fully read the weapon your wanting while frantically trying tobdodge demon's attacks ALL while being fast enough to keep on beat/ your multiplier.

But that's my only issues so far. The game is still great.

OG:- The game, from how it looks, plays (generally),  and (definitely) sounds is amazing overall.
Since I'm not the best at fast paced shooters, 75% blind and with only my left eye, and would have trouble keeping beat all while dodging and attacking at max speed; I am BEYOND thankful there is a auto "stay on beat" option to use so I can basically play it like  a basic bullet-hell (😂) game with the added absolutely amazing soundtrack. It takes away your scores on the leaderboards, but I have never cared about leaderboards at all, so that's completely fine. I've no need for them.

My only big issue I've come across so far is not being able to switch gun hands in-game. Because even in the main menu, the only option is to basically reverse the left and right controller button layouts almost completely...
This isn't a problem besides the ONE weapon I PAID EXTRA MONEY FOR- the bow & arrow/  Purgatory! With me only having my left eye and being very dominantly right handed, this has made it to where I use bows in my right hand while arrows are aimed/ shot with my left due to sight. In all other VR games I own this is fine, because you can freely switch hands manually.
In this I can't without reversing all of the controls!...
I also can't get refunds for this $7 Add-On with an unusable weapon for me, so da*n me for giving into it and buying I guess..
Mostly asted money thanks to this design oversight, because I otherwise wouldn't have spent even more money I barelyhave at the moment if I'd had known it worked this way!

...But the overall game (outside of that massive blunder) is great and I still recommend it.
Just don't buy the Purgatory Add-On if you like/ need to use your right hand to hold the bow...

Look…I get that VR is not the easiest technology to develop…but it’s achievable on stand-alone headsets. 

As a business analyst, I go into games wondering what kind of UAT and QA testing were done before implementing the final product. When I get a $30 game, I expect that proper R&D went into it along with rigorous testing. 

I have the Quest 3 standalone…and have to say I think the graphics look ok…and the music sounds great…I like the intro safehouse like area before starting a mission, cool concept…but the gameplay is terrible and janky. 

Keeping beat is easy with the guns, but using the sword is janky and you lose tempo easily due to mis-hits because of the hand tracking. Then when you’re able to do the finisher move, it simply teleports you at the enemy and they die, your sword that appears briefly is useless during that moment too, which takes away from the game altogether.

All in all, I am not sure if stand-alone headsets were even tested, but the final product has me pretty sure they weren’t. I’m sure after a few updates the game will play better but too many big title games have been releasing on VR and have too many issues, while the Quest 3 was boasted to be graphically superior to it’s predecessor, these games look like Quest 2 port overs…not built from the ground up for Quest 3.

reviews.review_title: Best time I've had in a long time
reviews.review_description:
I hadn't heard of  the Hellsinger franchise before I saw the trailer one day on the quest store. Being a huge fan of rhythm game like Pistol Whip and RagnaRock, this game definitely caught my eye. And after much waiting, it's here..and I love the poopoo out of it✨️😀✨️
 it makes you feel like a bad boi, killing demons to the beat of some heavy tracks. I was slightly worried that the music might not be what im into, but the soundtrack on this game fits perfectly. My type of game. I see a few  people complaining about bugs and glitches, but I haven't experienced any of that and it's been smooth sailing so far on my quest 3. Dont get me wrong, I'm not saying this i perfect game. Like all games, there are a couple things to complain about. There could be better button mapping and the sword is janky to use, along with the boomarangs. But for me, those things dont make this game anything short of incredibly fun. I dunno, in my opinion this game is sick af. I say buy it if you like rhythm games and slaying demons🤷‍♀️

"Metal Hellsinger VR is an intense thrill ride that rewards rhythmic blasting, but never forces it. I do wish that it went all in with the immersive controls to take advantage of VR, but the game is so enjoyable that it’s also quite easy to forgive."

Read the full review over at The Elite Institute (dot) net!
This game is so much fun! Pretty decent graphics, fantastic gameplay, and hands down, the best soundtrack in any game I ever played. Absolutely a blast!

Oh wow! 

What a game! I love the way the music aligns perfectly with the combat! Sooooo cool. 

Amazing knocked it out the park! 

This is worth every penny and more! 

Just WOW

This game is the first VR title giving me headaches. Not from the movement (which on its own is perfectly done), but from how sluggish/ghosting the reticle can be. It is almost impossible to hit something and catch the right beat. Until fixed, this game is on a backburner... (played on Q3)

I waited to get the preorder stuff and I didnt get it, so I went to my email and saw that it cancelled my preorder on it's own even though I had the quest balance to preorder and buy it, so can you guys who made the game give me the preorder stuff plz

reviews.review_title: Great game but the graphics are potato
reviews.review_description: I’m a bit stunned how pixelated it looks on Meta Quest 2. Could we get a texture pack or something? My cellphone could run these graphics.
Glitches everywhere, uncomfortable to play.

As some of the reviews mention, there are a few things that could be improved/polished, but pretty much every vr game launch is like that. Other than that, this game is fantastic and is so much fun to play. As a musician, I find the whole rhythm based combat system fantastic, and it makes everything feel so much cooler and more impressive. I would highly recommend it to anyone wondering. My advice to the developers if they see this, well first of all, fantastic job everyone, second, if the melee combat and finishers could be made a little more physics based/visceral feeling this game would be near perfect. As of now, like many other vr games, it feels kind of detached, like you are using a lightsaber, but if the melee felt like it had weight to it and was more, for lack of a better word, meaty, it would feel incredible

...please one thing: a quest 3 graphics update would be really great! 
The graphics are not bad at all. The fights are fast and intense so you can't really focus on details but when you move from one setting to the other you can see a few spots that look not perfect. But we're talking here about really small things.

Love the really smooth Gameplay!
The weapons feel great!
The Sound is fantastic!
The voice of Paz and the pictures of the storyline are awesome!

You guys did a great job. 👍 

I also loved the PC Version.
